title: Unsolicited SMS in GoHighLevel — Why Your Numbers Are Getting Banned
description: Sending cold SMS through GoHighLevel without consent? Your numbers will get banned. Here's why unsolicited texting violates TCPA and A2P 10DLC rules, and what to do instead.
html_content:
The Problem With Cold SMS in GoHighLevel

Every week, another agency or investor gets their GHL phone numbers banned. The story's always the same: they pulled a skip-traced list, loaded it into GoHighLevel, and blasted out hundreds or thousands of unsolicited text messages.

Then their numbers get flagged. Carrier filtering kicks in. Messages stop delivering. Eventually the numbers are banned entirely. They buy new numbers, do it again, and the cycle repeats — except now carriers are watching their account.

This isn't a GoHighLevel problem. GHL is just the platform. The problem is sending unsolicited SMS to people who never gave you permission to text them.

Why Your GHL Phone Numbers Are Getting Banned

There are three layers of enforcement that catch unsolicited SMS:

1. TCPA — Federal Law

The Telephone Consumer Protection Act makes it illegal to send marketing text messages to someone without their prior express written consent. Violations carry fines of $500–$1,500 per message. Class action lawsuits against SMS spammers routinely result in six- and seven-figure settlements.

2. A2P 10DLC Registration in GoHighLevel

Since 2023, all business SMS (Application-to-Person) sent through 10-digit long codes must be registered through the A2P 10DLC system. Carriers (T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon) review your use case, and your messages are filtered based on your trust score. Sending unsolicited messages tanks your trust score and gets your campaign suspended.

If you're using GoHighLevel with A2P 10DLC registration and sending cold SMS, you're putting your entire account at risk — not just one number.

3. Carrier Filtering

Even if you somehow avoid TCPA enforcement, carriers run their own spam detection. They look at:

  • Message volume spikes from new numbers
  • High opt-out rates
  • Spam complaint rates
  • Message content patterns that look like bulk marketing
  • Low engagement (no replies to your messages)

When any of these trigger, your number gets flagged. Enough flags and it's permanently banned.

Why Real Estate Investors Are the Worst Offenders

The real estate investing community has a cold outreach culture. Skip tracing services sell phone numbers for distressed property owners, and investors blast them with "Are you interested in selling your property?" texts.

This worked for a brief window in 2020–2021 when enforcement was lax. It doesn't work anymore. Carriers have implemented aggressive filtering specifically targeting this pattern. The REI cold SMS playbook is now a fast track to getting banned.

If you're in real estate investing and using GoHighLevel for cold SMS, it's not a question of whether your numbers will get banned. It's a question of when.

What Happens When Your Numbers Get Banned

When a number is banned by a carrier:

  • Messages silently fail — they show as sent in GHL but never arrive
  • Your other numbers get scrutinized — carriers flag the account, not just the number
  • New numbers on the same account get filtered faster — you can't just buy more numbers and start over
  • Your A2P 10DLC registration can be revoked — losing the ability to send any business SMS through GoHighLevel
  • GHL can suspend your sub-account — they enforce compliance too

What to Do Instead — Consent-Based SMS That Actually Converts

The businesses seeing the best results with GoHighLevel SMS aren't doing cold outreach. They're building systems that generate opt-in leads and then nurturing them with smart automation.

Inbound Lead Capture

Run Google Ads, Facebook Ads, or content marketing that drives people to landing pages where they voluntarily submit their phone number. They're opting in. You have consent. Your messages deliver.

AI-Powered Follow-Up

Once someone opts in, use AI conversation bots in GoHighLevel to respond to them personally. These aren't generic drip sequences — they're tailored messages that qualify leads and book appointments. Response rates are significantly higher than cold SMS because the person actually asked to hear from you.

Content-to-Lead Pipelines

Publish content (podcasts, blogs, videos) that establishes your expertise. People who consume your content and then opt in are warm leads. They already trust you. Follow-up SMS to these contacts tends to convert at much higher rates than cold outreach — we've seen it firsthand.

Review and Referral Automation

After completing a job or transaction, automate review requests and referral campaigns via SMS. These contacts have a real relationship with you. They won't report you as spam.

The Math: Cold SMS vs Consent-Based

Cold SMS looks cheap until you factor in the real costs:

  • Skip tracing lists: ~$0.05–0.15 per record
  • Replacement phone numbers when banned: $1–2 each, over and over
  • Time spent managing banned numbers and re-registering
  • Risk of TCPA lawsuit: $500–1,500 per message
  • Typical cold SMS conversion rates are well under 1%

Consent-based SMS has higher upfront cost (you need to run ads or create content to generate opt-ins) but:

  • Your numbers don't get banned
  • Messages actually deliver
  • Response rates are significantly higher when people expect to hear from you
  • Zero TCPA liability
  • Builds on itself as your content library and reputation grow

How We Build Compliant SMS Automation in GoHighLevel

At GlobalHighLevel, every SMS automation system we build is opt-in only. We don't support cold outreach, skip-traced lists, or unsolicited messaging.

We set up inbound lead systems inside your GoHighLevel account — AI chatbots, smart follow-up sequences, and booking automations that work with people who actually want to hear from you.

The result: better conversion rates, zero compliance risk, and phone numbers that keep working.

See our automation services or start a free 30-day GoHighLevel trial to build your own.

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney for guidance on TCPA compliance and SMS marketing regulations specific to your situation.
